heading: Election of mark to market for marketable stock
label: 1296 Election of mark to market for marketable stock
notes: References in TextSection 11A of the Securities and Exchange Act of 1934, referred to in subsec. (e)(1)(A)(i), is classified to , Commerce and Trade.section 78k–1 of Title 15
Prior ProvisionsA prior section 1296 was renumbered .section 1297 of this title

Amendments2010—Subsec. (i). amended subsec. (i) to read as if amendment by , had never been enacted. See 2001 Amendment note below.Pub. L. 111–312Pub. L. 107–16, § 542(e)(5)(C)2004—Subsec. (h). substituted “section 851(b)(2)” for “paragraphs (2) and (3) of section 851(b)”.Pub. L. 108–3112001—Subsec. (i). , struck out subsec. (i). Text read as follows: “In the case of stock of a passive foreign investment company which is acquired by bequest, devise, or inheritance (or by the decedent’s estate) and with respect to which an election under this section was in effect as of the date of the decedent’s death, notwithstanding section 1014, the basis of such stock in the hands of the person so acquiring it shall be the adjusted basis of such stock in the hands of the decedent immediately before his death (or, if lesser, the basis which would have been determined under section 1014 without regard to this subsection).”Pub. L. 107–16, § 542(e)(5)(C)1998—Subsec. (d). inserted at end “In the case of a regulated investment company which elected to mark to market the stock held by such company as of the last day of the taxable year preceding such company’s first taxable year for which such company elects the application of this section, the amount referred to in paragraph (1) shall include amounts included in gross income under such mark to market with respect to such stock for prior taxable years.”Pub. L. 105–206

children.text: If the fair market value of such stock as of the close of such taxable year exceeds its adjusted basis, such United States person shall include in gross income for such taxable year an amount equal to the amount of such excess.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount of such excess, or

children.label: B
children.text: the unreversed inclusions with respect to such stock.

children.label: A
children.text: shall be increased by the amount included in the gross income of the United States person under subsection (a)(1) with respect to such stock, and

children.label: B
children.text: shall be decreased by the amount allowed as a deduction to the United States person under subsection (a)(2) with respect to such stock.

children.heading: Special rule for stock constructively owned
children.label: 2 Special rule for stock constructively owned
A
children.text: the adjustments under paragraph (1) shall apply to such stock in the hands of the person actually holding such stock but only for purposes of determining the subsequent treatment under this chapter of the United States person with respect to such stock, and

children.label: B
children.text: similar adjustments shall be made to the adjusted basis of the property by reason of which the United States person is treated as owning such stock.

children.heading: Gain
children.label: A Gain
children.text: Any amount included in gross income under subsection (a)(1), and any gain on the sale or other disposition of marketable stock in a passive foreign investment company (with respect to which an election under this section is in effect), shall be treated as ordinary income.

children.label: i
children.text: amount allowed as a deduction under subsection (a)(2), and

children.label: ii
children.text: loss on the sale or other disposition of marketable stock in a passive foreign investment company (with respect to which an election under this section is in effect) to the extent that the amount of such loss does not exceed the unreversed inclusions with respect to such stock,

children.heading: Source
children.label: 2 Source
children.text: The source of any amount included in gross income under subsection (a)(1) (or allowed as a deduction under subsection (a)(2)) shall be determined in the same manner as if such amount were gain or loss (as the case may be) from the sale of stock in the passive foreign investment company.
the amount included in gross income of the taxpayer under subsection (a)(1) with respect to such stock for prior taxable years, over
the amount allowed as a deduction under subsection (a)(2) with respect to such stock for prior taxable years.

children.label: i
children.text: a national securities exchange which is registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission or the national market system established pursuant to section 11A of the Securities and Exchange Act of 1934, or

children.label: ii
children.text: any exchange or other market which the Secretary determines has rules adequate to carry out the purposes of this part,

children.label: B
children.text: to the extent provided in regulations, stock in any foreign corporation which is comparable to a regulated investment company and which offers for sale or has outstanding any stock of which it is the issuer and which is redeemable at its net asset value, and

children.label: C
children.text: to the extent provided in regulations, any option on stock described in subparagraph (A) or (B).

children.heading: Special rule for regulated investment companies
children.label: 2 Special rule for regulated investment companies
children.text: In the case of any regulated investment company which is offering for sale or has outstanding any stock of which it is the issuer and which is redeemable at its net asset value, all stock in a passive foreign investment company which it owns directly or indirectly shall be treated as marketable stock for purposes of this section. Except as provided in regulations, similar treatment as marketable stock shall apply in the case of any other regulated investment company which publishes net asset valuations at least annually.

children.heading: Treatment of controlled foreign corporations which are shareholders in passive foreign investment companies
children.label: f Treatment of controlled foreign corporations which are shareholders in passive foreign investment companies

children.text: this section (other than subsection (c)(2)) shall apply to such foreign corporation in the same manner as if such corporation were a United States person, and

children.label: A
children.text: any amount included in gross income under subsection (a)(1) shall be treated as foreign personal holding company income described in section 954(c)(1)(A), and

children.label: B
children.text: any amount allowed as a deduction under subsection (a)(2) shall be treated as a deduction allocable to foreign personal holding company income so described.

children.heading: Stock owned through certain foreign entities
children.label: g Stock owned through certain foreign entities

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of this section, stock owned, directly or indirectly, by or for a foreign partnership or foreign trust or foreign estate shall be considered as being owned proportionately by its partners or beneficiaries. Stock considered to be owned by a person by reason of the application of the preceding sentence shall, for purposes of applying such sentence, be treated as actually owned by such person.

children.label: A
children.text: any disposition by the United States person or by any other person which results in the United States person being treated as no longer owning such stock, and

children.label: B
children.text: any disposition by the person owning such stock,

children.heading: Coordination with section 851(b)
children.label: h Coordination with section 851(b)
children.text: For purposes of section 851(b)(2), any amount included in gross income under subsection (a) shall be treated as a dividend.

children.heading: Stock acquired from a decedent
children.label: i Stock acquired from a decedent
children.text: In the case of stock of a passive foreign investment company which is acquired by bequest, devise, or inheritance (or by the decedent’s estate) and with respect to which an election under this section was in effect as of the date of the decedent’s death, notwithstanding section 1014, the basis of such stock in the hands of the person so acquiring it shall be the adjusted basis of such stock in the hands of the decedent immediately before his death (or, if lesser, the basis which would have been determined under section 1014 without regard to this subsection).

children.heading: Coordination with section 1291 for first year of election
children.label: j Coordination with section 1291 for first year of election

children.heading: Taxpayers other than regulated investment companies
children.label: 1 Taxpayers other than regulated investment companies
i
children.text: any distributions with respect to, or disposition of, such stock in the first taxable year of the taxpayer for which such election is made, and

children.label: ii
children.text: any amount which, but for section 1291, would have been included in gross income under subsection (a) with respect to such stock for such taxable year in the same manner as if such amount were gain on the disposition of such stock.

children.heading: Requirements
children.label: B Requirements
children.text: The requirements of this subparagraph are met if, with respect to each of such corporation’s taxable years for which such corporation was a passive foreign investment company and which begin after , and included any portion of the taxpayer’s holding period in such stock, such corporation was treated as a qualified electing fund under this part with respect to the taxpayer.December 31, 1986

children.heading: Special rules for regulated investment companies
children.label: 2 Special rules for regulated investment companies
i
children.text: section 1291 shall not apply to such stock with respect to any distribution or disposition during, or amount included in gross income under this section for, such first taxable year, but

children.label: ii
children.text: such regulated investment company’s tax under this chapter for such first taxable year shall be increased by the aggregate amount of interest which would have been determined under section 1291(c)(3) if section 1291 were applied without regard to this subparagraph.

children.heading: Disallowance of deduction
children.label: B Disallowance of deduction
children.text: No deduction shall be allowed to any regulated investment company for the increase in tax under subparagraph (A)(ii).
such stock ceases to be marketable stock, or
the Secretary consents to the revocation of such election.

children.heading: Transition rule for individuals becoming subject to United States tax
children.label: l Transition rule for individuals becoming subject to United States tax
children.text: If any individual becomes a United States person in a taxable year beginning after , solely for purposes of this section, the adjusted basis (before adjustments under subsection (b)) of any marketable stock in a passive foreign investment company owned by such individual on the first day of such taxable year shall be treated as being the greater of its fair market value on such first day or its adjusted basis on such first day.December 31, 1997
